factors.summary: The company generated positive free cash flow, with an increase compared to the previous year.

evidence.text: We generated $100.4 million and $14.4 million of cash flow from operations during the three months ended March 31, 2015 and 2014. During the three months ended March 31, 2015, our cash flow from operations increased $86.0 million compared to the three months ended March 31, 2014.

factors.summary: The company is actively returning capital to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends.

evidence.text: We used $57.3 million and $52.4 million to pay cash dividends during the three months ended March 31, 2015 and 2014. We used $37.9 million and $2.0 million on share repurchases during the three months ended March 31, 2015 and 2014.

factors.summary: The company has a revolving credit facility in place, with no immediate signs of debt leverage issues.

evidence.text: The outstanding balance on the revolving credit facility was $630.0 million as of March 31, 2015. We were in compliance with all of the credit facility's financial debt covenants as of March 31, 2015.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are focused on information technology to improve efficiencies and support growth.

evidence.text: We used $6.7 million and $12.6 million for capital expenditures during the three months ended March 31, 2015 and 2014 primarily for investments in information technology equipment to support our operating systems, including the purchase and development of software.

factors.summary: Pricing power is not explicitly addressed, but the company is managing costs and improving efficiencies.

evidence.text: These information technology investments are intended to improve efficiencies and help grow the business.

factors.summary: Overall demand is growing, driven by increased volumes in most transportation modes.

evidence.text: Total Transportation revenues increased 5.0 percent to $2.9 billion in the first quarter of 2015 from $2.8 billion in the first quarter of 2014. The increase was driven by our acquisition of Freightquote.com and increased volumes in most of our transportation modes offset partially by lower rates charged to our customers, primarily due to the lower cost of fuel.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in LTL and air transportation, which saw significant increases in net revenue margins.

evidence.text: Our LTL net revenues increased 42.0 percent to $85.4 million in the first quarter of 2015 from $60.1 million in the first quarter of 2014. The increase was primarily due to increased volumes and net revenue margin. [...] Our air transportation net revenues increased 18.2 percent to $20.6 million in the first quarter of 2015 from $17.5 million in the first quarter of 2014. The increase was primarily due to increased volumes and net revenue margin.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Volumes are increasing across most transportation modes, with significant growth in LTL and air transportation.

evidence.text: LTL volumes increased approximately 28 percent in the first quarter of 2015 compared to the first quarter of 2014. [...] Our air transportation net revenues increased 18.2 percent to $20.6 million in the first quarter of 2015 from $17.5 million in the first quarter of 2014. The increase was primarily due to increased volumes and net revenue margin.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by lower fuel costs and improved net revenue margins in higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: Our Transportation net revenue margin increased to 16.8 percent in the first quarter of 2015 from 15.3 percent in the first quarter of 2014. [...] Income from operations increased 15.9 percent to $181.9 million in the first quarter of 2015 from $157.0 million in the first quarter of 2014. Income from operations as a percentage of net revenues increased slightly to 34.6 percent in the first quarter of 2015 from 34.3 percent in the first quarter of 2014.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Transportation business, due to lower fuel costs and efficient use of capacity.

evidence.text: Truckload net revenue margin increased in the first quarter of 2015 compared to the first quarter of 2014, due primarily to the lower cost of fuel. [...] Our LTL net revenues increased 42.0 percent to $85.4 million in the first quarter of 2015 from $60.1 million in the first quarter of 2014. The increase was primarily due to increased volumes and net revenue margin.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Market share is stable with a diverse customer base and no significant concentration, though the company is expanding its reach through acquisitions.

evidence.text: Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated. Our top 100 customers represented approximately 37 percent of our total revenues and approximately 26 percent of our net revenues in 2014. [...] In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc., which enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying with new entrants and aggressive rivals, but the company is differentiating through its global network and strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: Our branch office network is a competitive advantage. Building local customer and contract carrier relationships has been an important part of our success, and our worldwide network of offices supports our core strategy of serving customers locally, nationally, and globally. [...] In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc., which enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is low, with a diverse base, reducing the risk of dependency on a few large customers.

evidence.text: Our customer base is very diverse and unconcentrated. Our top 100 customers represented approximately 37 percent of our total revenues and approximately 26 percent of our net revenues in 2014. Our largest customer was approximately two percent of our total revenues and approximately one percent of our total net revenues in 2014.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value by expanding capabilities and driving synergies, as evidenced by the Freightquote acquisition.

evidence.text: In January 2015, we completed our acquisition of Freightquote.com, Inc. [...] The acquisition enhances and brings synergies to our LTL and truckload businesses, and expands our e-commerce capabilities.

factors.summary: The company is pursuing organic growth, supported by strategic investments in working capital and capital expenditures. However, this growth has been partially offset by lower rates due to fuel cost reductions, though the acquisition of Freightquote has contributed significantly to the increase in transportation revenues.

evidence.text: We prioritize our investments to grow the business, as we require some working capital and a relatively small amount of capital expenditures to grow.

Total Transportation revenues increased 5.0 percent in the first quarter of 2015 compared to the first quarter of 2014. [...] The increase was driven by our acquisition of Freightquote.com and increased volumes in most of our transportation modes offset partially by lower rates charged to our customers, primarily due to the lower cost of fuel.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS
